format_discipline.notes: reading.md first non-empty line is the H1 '# Knowing which governance framework applies to your system'; no leading YAML. reading.meta.json exists, parses, carries word_count/estimated_read_minutes/citations_used/sections. Exactly the six fixed sections in order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References (### sub-headings under Key Concepts are permitted).

length.notes: 1290 words in [1200,1600]. 7 min via canonical artifact-mix reading_wpm=200 (ceil(1290/200)=7) in [6,8]. SKILL's words/230 figure is stale relative to the canonical config; used canonical 200 wpm.

citations.notes: All three references cited inline and present in footnote-style bibliography; no orphans. EU AI Act scope [1] cited for the extraterritorial-reach claim (lines 25, 30, 52, 69, 79). Back-refs to prior 5.x topics are narrative pointers, not citations, which is correct for a synthesis topic.

forward_references.notes: 5.13 is the last topic in week 5. All cross-refs (5.4, 5.5, 5.7, 5.10, 5.11, 5.12) are back-refs.

beginner_readability.notes: jurisdiction defined on first use (line 13, bold-term/plain-meaning). extraterritorial reach defined on first use (line 25, bold-term/plain-meaning). Acronyms (NIST AI RMF, MEITY, EO 14110) carry context. Concrete-first, plain register, no jargon dumps, no hedging.

formatting.notes: Sequences rendered as numbered/bulleted lists; no walls of text; inline examples (spam filter, hiring tool) present alongside concepts; definitions in bold-term shape. Diagram embedded at line 18 with valid relative ref './diagram.png'; file exists (non-empty). No broken image ref.

scope.notes: SYNTHESIS-SPECIFIC: teaches the DECISION (two-question method + decision flowchart) and REFERENCES the four frameworks by name plus back-ref. Does NOT re-teach framework internals at depth: NIST Govern/Map/Measure/Manage, EO 14110 directives, and MEITY mechanics are named and deferred, not unpacked. EU risk tiers (lines 36-39) are recalled from 5.7 as the basis for Question 2, not re-taught from scratch. Compliance procedures and certification bodies named-and-deferred only (line 73: 'named here only so you know they exist'). No drift into 5.7/5.10/5.11/5.12.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights in learner prose (A2 metadata lives only in topic.manifest.json). No script/meta artifacts (Hands-On Exercise, Next Steps, numbered corpus headings) leaked into the reading.

sampled: design-time engineering decision, like a database/security standard -> corpus sec 4 / 5.5
two questions: jurisdiction + risk level -> corpus 5.1
EU AI Act extraterritorial reach, output used in EU -> corpus 5.2
India hiring-tool worked example, three frameworks at once -> corpus 5.4
EO 14110 revocable/revoked, MEITY revisable, EU AI Act binding -> corpus 5.6
compliance procedures + certification bodies are specialist work, deferred -> corpus 5.6
faithfulness.notes: All sampled assertions trace to the corpus; no invented claims. Threshold is >2 unsupported -> fail; found 0.

gold_example_note: Gold reading examples exist (.claude/references/gold-examples/reading/2-1-..., 3-4-...). This reading meets that bar: clean H1-first format, six-section template, bold-term definitions, scannable lists, inline examples, beginner register.
verdict: PASS. 5.13 synthesis reading clears all reading-critic checks: clean format (no frontmatter, valid sidecar, six sections in order), 1290 words / 7 min in range, citations clean with EU AI Act scope [1] backing the extraterritorial-reach claim, all back-refs (no forward refs), faithful to corpus (0 unsupported), and scope-disciplined as a synthesis topic - it teaches the decision and references the four frameworks without re-teaching their internals. jurisdiction and extraterritorial reach are both defined on first use; compliance procedures and certification bodies are named-and-deferred only. Diagram embedded with a valid, non-broken image ref. No assessment prose, no script/meta artifacts.
